mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so a sustained easing path predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive in easing periods, or if the signal's rolling 2-year rank correlation falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A positive monthly rise in RSAFS above recent trend indicates stronger consumer spending, which lifts near-term revenue expectations and supports S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered RSAFS strength is followed by negative or zero 5-day SPX returns in more than 60% of observations, or if conditional mean return is not above unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating UNRATE increase marks labor-market deterioration and rising recession risk, which should depress equity prices and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment acceleration is not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate of negative returns stays below 55% in triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO and PAYEMS both strengthen together, the economy is expanding without a sharp demand shock, which supports cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvements in INDPRO and PAYEMS do not outperform single-series signals, or if 60-day SPX returns are not positive in the top interaction quartile.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals housing demand weakening and a coming slowdown in household activity, which should pull S&P 500 returns lower over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month trend by more than 10%
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: An inflation surprise when CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS stays near the zero lower bound compresses real policy accommodation and hurts equities, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 2 percentage points
regime_hint: high inflation, low policy rate regime
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-pressure episodes are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power versus CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 growth during a recovery phase indicates improving aggregate demand and earnings breadth, which should deliver positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarterly GDPC1 growth does not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal becomes negative in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and a stable or falling UNRATE identifies labor-market resilience, which supports household income expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases while UNRATE does not rise over the same month
failure_condition: Reject if labor-resilience episodes are followed by non-positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if PAYEMS and UNRATE together do not outperform either series alone.
